title: mcp-fit A2A Agent Card Scorecard
description: Machine-readable Agent Card scorecard emitted by mcp-fit as card-compat.json. Validates against the ADR-F contract. Independent of compat.schema.json — the MCP scorecard contract is frozen.

schemaVersion.description: Card-scorecard schema version. Bump on breaking changes.

axes.description: Per-axis scores over the seven card-usability axes (ADR-F2). All deterministic.

card-completeness.description: REQUIRED-field presence per A2A proto field_behavior annotations.

skill-namespacing.description: Skill name/description discoverability (transferred from MCP namespacing).

skill-selection-overlap.description: Pairwise skill id/name/tag ambiguity (transferred from MCP tool-selection-confusion, statically gradable on cards).

signature-hygiene.description: JWS signature presence + structural validity (A2A spec section 8.4).

security-declaration-consistency.description: Security requirements resolve to declared schemes; schemes carry REQUIRED subfields.

extension-hygiene.description: Extension URI validity, descriptions, required:true surfacing (spec section 4.4.4).

interface-hygiene.description: Interface URL absoluteness/HTTPS, known protocol bindings, versions (spec section 5.2).

aggregate.description: Weighted aggregate over all seven axes (ADR-F3 weights).

lintScore.description: Deterministic headline — every card axis is deterministic, so this is the badge.

weighted.description: Identical to lintScore in v1 (no eval split on the card side).

skills.description: Per-skill findings list, keyed by skill id (name fallback).

CardMeta.description: Identity of the scored Agent Card.

name.description: Card name ('unknown' when absent from the card).

version.description: Card version ('unknown' when absent from the card).

score.description: 1-10 ordinal; never null — every card axis is statically gradable.

kind.description: Card axes are always deterministic in v1.

skill.description: Offending skill id (or name fallback), if applicable.

SignatureReport.description: Structural signature analysis (ADR-F4). The 'structural' tier never claims cryptographic validity.

tier.description: Highest tier PASSED; null when absent or structurally invalid. Crypto tiers are deferred (ADR-F4).
